message:
fix(channel): emit clock skew evidence for all skew types, prevent jitter zero-collapse at 1ns boundary

Channel clock skew subsystem fixes across 2 files:

src/channel/clock_skew.rs — Introduced ComputedSkew struct replacing raw
SkewNanos return from compute_skew. Evidence emission moved from inside
compute_skew to the caller now() method so it fires for all skew types
(static offset, drift, jitter), not just jumps. Previously, static offsets
and jitter-only configurations would silently produce skewed timestamps
without any evidence trail. Jitter magnitude changed from (random % max)
to (random % max) + 1 to prevent zero-collapse when jitter_max_ns == 1,
which would otherwise produce no jitter at all despite being configured.
Added 2 tests: static_offset_emits_skew_evidence validates evidence is
recorded for static offset reads, one_nanosecond_jitter_does_not_collapse_to_zero
validates the boundary fix.

tests/channel_clock_skew_injection.rs — Updated jitter bound assertion
from strict less-than to less-than-or-equal to account for the +1 fix.
Added one_nanosecond_jitter_produces_real_skew integration test. Added
evidence_logged_for_static_offset_reads integration test verifying evidence
emission through the full injection pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
